indicator_text: Lobbying is a legitimate activity and an important part of the democratic process.
source_quote: Lobbying is a legitimate activity and an important part of the democratic process. papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:103
notes: Unnumbered guiding principle (preamble); not part of the 38 numbered standards.

indicator_text: There is a significant public interest in ensuring the transparency and integrity of lobbying, as well as diversity of participation and contribution to public decision-making.
source_quote: There is a significant public interest in ensuring the transparency and integrity of lobbying, as well as diversity of participation and contribution to public decision-making. papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:105-106

indicator_text: Any regulatory measures shall be proportionate, fit for purpose and not impede on the individual rights of assembly, free speech and petition of government.
source_quote: Any regulatory measures to secure these ends shall be proportionate, fit for purpose and not impede on the individual rights of assembly, free speech and petition of government. papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:108-109

indicator_text: 'Lobbying' should cover any direct or indirect communication with a public official made, managed or directed with the purpose of influencing public decision-making.
source_quote: 'Lobbying' – the term should cover any direct or indirect communication with a public official that is made, managed or directed with the purpose of influencing public decision-making. papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:121-123

indicator_text: 'Public Official' shall include any individual with decision-making powers (and their advisors), elected/appointed/employed in executive or legislative branches at national, sub-national, or supra-national levels; in private bodies performing public functions; and in public international organisations domiciled or operational in the country.
source_quote: 'Public Official' – shall include any individual with decision-making powers (and their advisors), who are elected, appointed or employed within the executive or legislative branches papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:125-129

indicator_text: 'Lobbyist' should entail any natural or legal person who engages in lobbying activities, whether for private, public or collective ends, whether for compensation or without.
source_quote: 'Lobbyist' – should entail any natural or legal person who engages in lobbying activities, whether for private, public or collective ends, whether for compensation or without. papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:131-132

indicator_text: 'Public decision-making' shall include creation/amendment of legislation or other regulatory measures; development, modification and implementation of public policies, strategies and programmes; and awarding of government contracts/grants, administrative decisions or other public spending decisions.
source_quote: 'Public decision-making' – shall include the creation and amendment of legislation or any other regulatory measures; the development, modification and implementation of public policies papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:134-137

indicator_text: Citizen interactions concerning private affairs shall not be considered lobbying, save where they may concern individual economic interests of sufficient size or importance to potentially compromise public interest; in such case a balancing act on benefit and efficacy of regulation is needed, with consideration for constitutional protections.
source_quote: Citizen interactions – the interaction of individual citizens with public officials concerning their private affairs shall not be considered lobbying, save for where it may concern individual economic interests of sufficient size or importance papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:141-146

indicator_text: If deemed necessary, the regulation may exclude public officials acting in their official capacity, diplomatic agents of foreign states, and political parties (since they play a quasi-public role).
source_quote: Public officials, diplomats, political parties – if deemed necessary, the regulation may exclude public officials acting in their official capacity, diplomatic agents of foreign states and political parties (since they play a quasi-public role). papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:148-150

indicator_text: Mandatory register: framework should require establishment of a mandatory lobbying register to which lobbyists, and all organisations who lobby (in-house or other), must sign up; activities are reported on a periodic basis along with designated supplementary information.
source_quote: Mandatory register – the framework should require the establishment of a mandatory lobbying register to which the lobbyists, and all organisations who lobby whether by employing in-house or other lobbyists, must sign up papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:165-169

indicator_text: Timely registration and reporting: initial lobbyist registration should take place before any lobbying activity; updates shortly after relevant changes; activity reporting frequency set to allow meaningful analysis (minimum quarterly, ideally close to real-time).
source_quote: Timely registration and reporting – the initial lobbyist registration should take place in a timely fashion, before any lobbying activity takes place... (minimum quarterly, ideally close to real-time). papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:171-175

indicator_text: Information disclosed: among others, the register shall include information on lobbyist identity, subject matter and outcomes sought, ultimate beneficiary, targeted institution/official, type and frequency of activities, supporting documentation, expenditure (incl. in-kind), funding sources, political contributions, prior public-official roles (incl. family), and public funding received.
source_quote: Information disclosed – among others, the register shall include information on: papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:177
notes: Top-level standard #9 of 38. Composite indicator broken into 11 granular sub-points (3.a–3.k).

indicator_text: Lobbyist identity
source_quote: a. lobbyist identity papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:179

indicator_text: The subject matter of lobbying activities and outcomes sought
source_quote: b. the subject matter of lobbying activities and outcomes sought papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:181

indicator_text: The ultimate beneficiary of lobbying activities (where relevant)
source_quote: c. the ultimate beneficiary of lobbying activities (where relevant) papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:183

indicator_text: The targeted institution and/or the public official concerned
source_quote: d. the targeted institution and/or the public official concerned papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:185

indicator_text: The type and frequency of lobbying activities
source_quote: e. the type and frequency of lobbying activities papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:187

indicator_text: Any supporting documentation shared with the public officials
source_quote: f. any supporting documentation shared with the public officials papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:189

indicator_text: Lobbying expenditure, including in-kind (calculated to set criteria, in cost bands if need be)
source_quote: g. lobbying expenditure, including in-kind (calculated to set criteria, in cost bands, if need be) papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:191-192

indicator_text: Sources of funding, per client and per dossier
source_quote: h. sources of funding, per client and per dossier papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:194

indicator_text: Any political contributions, including in-kind
source_quote: i. any political contributions, including in-kind papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:196

indicator_text: Any prior roles as public official held by the individual and/or family members
source_quote: j. any prior roles as public official held by the individual and/or family members papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:198

indicator_text: Public funding received
source_quote: k. public funding received papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:200

indicator_text: Accessibility, openness and comparability of data: information online via single website, free of charge, indexable and downloadable in full as machine-readable open data (per opendefinition.org); should conform to existing open data standards; unique identifier per lobbyist/organisation; linking to other data sets is recommended.
scoring_rule: Presence + multiple sub-criteria (online, free, machine-readable, indexable, unique IDs, linked)
source_quote: Accessibility, openness and comparability of data – the information should be made available online, through a single website, free of charge, indexable and downloadable in full as machine readable open data papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:205-208

indicator_text: Minimal administrative burden: system should allow ease of registration and reporting and minimise burden; balance with public-sector proactive disclosure obligations and the public right to know.
scoring_rule: Qualitative — burden minimised + balanced with proactive disclosure
source_quote: Minimal administrative burden – the system should allow for ease of registration and reporting, and look to minimise the administrative burden involved. papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:213-216

indicator_text: Access to information law: a comprehensive access-to-information law shall guarantee the public's right of access to information, including information about lobbying.
source_quote: Access to information law – a comprehensive access to information law shall guarantee the public's right of access to information, including information about lobbying. papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:220-221

indicator_text: Limited exceptions: ATI law should set out a limited number of exceptions (privacy, security, financial sensitivity, decision-making process, other legitimate concerns), bound by a harm and public-interest test in line with international standards.
source_quote: Limited exceptions – the law should clearly set out a limited number of exceptions to address the privacy, security, financial sensitivity, decision-making process and any other legitimate concerns, which should be bound by a harm and public interest test papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:223-226

indicator_text: Proactive publication: public bodies/officials proactively publish organisational, programmatic, administrative, financial, and business-schedule information; meeting summaries; background documentation; preparatory analyses; obligations extend to expert/consultative bodies.
source_quote: Proactive publication – the public bodies and officials should proactively publish their organisational, programmatic, administrative, financial, and business schedule information, summaries of meetings and other interactions papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:228-233

indicator_text: Decision-making footprint: where possible, and certainly for all legislative and policy initiatives, information should be clustered around individual items, producing a 'decision-making footprint' outlining history, public engagement and process; should link to lobbying-register data.
source_quote: Decision-making footprint – where possible, and certainly for all legislative and policy initiatives, this information should be clustered around the individual items for consideration, producing a 'decision-making footprint' papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:235-239

indicator_text: Clear, free and comprehensive: all information made public without charge (excluding actual delivery costs) and without limits to reuse; key information accessible/comprehensible to citizens and interest groups; recently generated data published as machine-readable open data.
source_quote: Clear, Free and Comprehensive – all information must be made public without charge (excluding actual costs on delivery) and without limits to reuse. papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:241-245

indicator_text: Codes of Conduct (public officials): all public officials shall have an applicable set of rules laying out key standards of conduct including for dealings with third parties; rules may be institution-dependent but should include behavioural principles, record-keeping duty, duty to avoid unregistered lobbyists, confidentiality, conflict-of-interest mechanism, gifts/hospitality guidance, assets/interests disclosure.
source_quote: Codes of Conduct – all public officials shall have an applicable set of rules laying out the key standards of conduct, including for their dealings with third parties. papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:257-259
notes: Top-level standard #17 of 38. Composite indicator broken into 7 granular sub-points (1.a–1.g).

indicator_text: Key behavioural principles including transparency, integrity, responsiveness, impartiality, fairness, accountability and serving the public interest
section_or_category: INTEGRITY > Public Officials > Codes of Conduct
source_quote: a. Key behavioural principles including those of transparency, integrity, responsiveness, impartiality, fairness, accountability and serving the public interest papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:261-262

indicator_text: The duty to keep a true and detailed record of their actions, including of meetings with lobbyists
section_or_category: INTEGRITY > Public Officials > Codes of Conduct
source_quote: b. The duty to keep a true and detailed record of their actions, including of meetings with lobbyists papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:264-265

indicator_text: The duty to avoid lobbying contacts with unregistered lobbyists, and to report any violations of the lobbying rules to their superiors or relevant bodies
section_or_category: INTEGRITY > Public Officials > Codes of Conduct
source_quote: c. The duty to avoid lobbying contacts with unregistered lobbyists, and to report any violations of the lobbying rules to their superiors or relevant bodies papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:267-268

indicator_text: The duty of confidentiality, subject to the public access to information regime
section_or_category: INTEGRITY > Public Officials > Codes of Conduct
source_quote: d. The duty of confidentiality, subject to the public access to information regime papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:270

indicator_text: A comprehensive mechanism for dealing with any real, potential or apparent conflicts of interest, including the incompatibilities of being a lobbyist
section_or_category: INTEGRITY > Public Officials > Codes of Conduct
source_quote: e. A comprehensive mechanism for dealing with any real, potential or apparent conflicts of interest, including the incompatibilities of being a lobbyist papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:272-273

indicator_text: A comprehensive guidance for dealing with gifts and hospitality, including their registration or decline
section_or_category: INTEGRITY > Public Officials > Codes of Conduct
source_quote: f. A comprehensive guidance for dealing with gifts and hospitality, including their registration or decline papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:275-276

indicator_text: A system for assets and interests disclosure by the public official, as well as by their family and business partners depending on the scope of decision-making power
section_or_category: INTEGRITY > Public Officials > Codes of Conduct
source_quote: g. A system for assets and interests disclosure by the public official, as well as by their family and business partners depending on the scope of the decision-making power of the official. papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:278-280

indicator_text: Post-employment restrictions: proportionate moratoria (or 'cooling-off periods') of at least 2 years before former public officials can lobby their former institutions concerning prior duties; approval from a designated ethics agency may be required.
source_quote: Post-employment restrictions – there should be a proportionate moratoria (or 'cooling-off periods') of at least 2 years before former public officials can lobby their former institutions papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:282-285

indicator_text: Pre-employment restrictions: prospective public officials (excluding those elected), particularly those hired/seconded into advisory and regulatory roles, subject to a conflict-of-interest vetting process that may necessitate recusal, supervision, or disqualification; omission to declare relevant details should trigger disciplinary procedures.
source_quote: Pre-employment restrictions – prospective public officials, excluding those elected into office, but particularly those hired or seconded into advisory and regulatory roles, shall be subject to a conflicts of interest vetting process papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:291-296

indicator_text: Statutory code of conduct: there should be a statutory code of conduct for lobbyists developed in close consultation with all stakeholders and interested parties.
source_quote: Statutory code of conduct – there should be a statutory code of conduct for lobbyists developed in close consultation with all stakeholders and interested parties. papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:300-301

indicator_text: Behavioural standards: the code shall lay out core behavioural standards, particularly with regard to honesty and the avoidance of undue influence.
source_quote: Behavioural standards – the code shall lay out the core behavioural standards, particularly with regards to honesty and the avoidance of undue influence. papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:303-304

indicator_text: Self-regulation: supplementary measures shall be taken to encourage lobbyists to voluntarily adopt, publicise and report on additional ethical commitments, including through collective action; commitments backed by internal control/sanction mechanisms and integrated within CSR/governance strategy.
source_quote: Self-regulation – supplementary measures shall be taken to encourage lobbyists to voluntarily adopt, publicise and report on additional ethical commitments, including through collective action. papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:306-310

indicator_text: Right to participate: a generally recognised right for all groups and the public at large to participate in public decision-making, extending in particular to legislative and policy matters, within all levels of governance.
section_or_category: PARTICIPATION & ACCESS > Public Participation in Decision-making
source_quote: Right to participate – there should be a generally recognised right for all groups and public at large to participate in public decision-making papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:323-325

indicator_text: Public consultation process: a legal framework should lay out, in a law or group of laws, the varied means for public participation in formulation, implementation, and evaluation of policies and laws — including timeframes, mechanisms to disseminate meeting information, attendance/participation rules, and tools to submit comments.
section_or_category: PARTICIPATION & ACCESS > Public Participation in Decision-making
source_quote: Public consultation process – a legal framework should lay out in a law or a group of laws the varied means for public participation in the formulation, implementation, and evaluation of policies and laws papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:327-331

indicator_text: Equal opportunity: there shall be an obligation on public authorities to provide an equal opportunity for participation to various interest groups and the public at large.
section_or_category: PARTICIPATION & ACCESS > Public Participation in Decision-making
source_quote: Equal opportunity – there shall be an obligation on public authorities to provide an equal opportunity for participation to various interest groups and the public at large. papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:333-334

indicator_text: Timely and effective contribution: public authorities should provide an adequate consultation period allowing sufficient time for review of preparatory materials, and should promote effective participation at the appropriate stage while decisions are still open.
section_or_category: PARTICIPATION & ACCESS > Public Participation in Decision-making
source_quote: Timely and effective contribution – the public authorities should be obliged to provide an adequate time period for consultation allowing sufficient time for review of the preparatory materials papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:336-339

indicator_text: Publication of results: written and verbal views of consultation participants shall be made public; authorities should outline how the various views have been taken into account and why.
section_or_category: PARTICIPATION & ACCESS > Public Participation in Decision-making
source_quote: Publication of results – the (written and verbal) views of participants in the consultation process shall be made public, and the authorities should outline how the various views have been taken into account and why. papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:341-343

indicator_text: Refusals of the right: public authorities shall provide a written justification for any refusal of the right to participate; those excluded shall have options to challenge and contest the exclusion in a procedure expeditious enough to allow participation if the refusal is overturned.
section_or_category: PARTICIPATION & ACCESS > Public Participation in Decision-making
source_quote: Refusals of the right – the public authorities shall provide a written justification for any refusal of the right to participate, and those excluded shall have options available to challenge and contest that exclusion papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:345-348

indicator_text: Balanced composition: there should be a legal obligation on public authorities to strive for a balanced composition of expert and advisory bodies, representing a diversity of interests and views.
source_quote: Balanced composition – there should be a legal obligation on public authorities to strive for a balanced composition of expert and advisory bodies, representing a diversity of interests and views. papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:355-357

indicator_text: Disclosure of interests: advisory and expert group members shall be required to disclose their interests and affiliations relevant to items under consideration in advance of any work on the issue.
source_quote: Disclosure of interests – advisory and expert group members shall be required to disclose their interests and affiliations relevant to items under consideration in advance of any work on the issue. papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:359-361

indicator_text: Lobbyist incentives: any lobbyist incentives should be considered with care, so as not to entrench special privilege of organised interests over those of individual citizens.
source_quote: Lobbyist incentives – any lobbyist incentives should be considered with care, so as not to entrench special privilege of organised interests over those of individual citizens. papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:365-366

indicator_text: Management & Investigation: an independent, mandated and well-resourced oversight body or coordinated mechanism should be charged with managing registration, reviewing conflicts of interest, collating proactively disclosed information, monitoring compliance (incl. spot-audits), following up on complaints, investigating breaches, and consulting on/defining further provisions.
source_quote: Management & Investigation – an independent, mandated and well-resourced oversight body or coordinated mechanism should be charged with: papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:377-378
notes: Top-level standard #32 of 38. Composite indicator broken into 7 granular sub-points (1.a–1.g).

indicator_text: Managing lobbying registration
section_or_category: OVERSIGHT, MANAGEMENT AND SANCTIONS > Management & Investigation
source_quote: a. managing lobbying registration papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:380

indicator_text: Reviewing potential conflicts of interest
section_or_category: OVERSIGHT, MANAGEMENT AND SANCTIONS > Management & Investigation
source_quote: b. reviewing potential conflicts of interest papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:382

indicator_text: Collating and disseminating the locations of proactively disclosed information
section_or_category: OVERSIGHT, MANAGEMENT AND SANCTIONS > Management & Investigation
source_quote: c. collating and disseminating the locations of proactively disclosed information papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:384

indicator_text: Monitoring compliance (including pro-active verification and spot-audits of reports)
section_or_category: OVERSIGHT, MANAGEMENT AND SANCTIONS > Management & Investigation
source_quote: d. monitoring compliance (including pro-active verification and spot-audits of reports) papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:386

indicator_text: Following up on complaints
section_or_category: OVERSIGHT, MANAGEMENT AND SANCTIONS > Management & Investigation
source_quote: e. following up on complaints papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:388

indicator_text: Investigating apparent breaches and anomalies
section_or_category: OVERSIGHT, MANAGEMENT AND SANCTIONS > Management & Investigation
source_quote: f. investigating apparent breaches and anomalies papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:390

indicator_text: Consulting on and defining further regulatory provisions where there is a mandate
section_or_category: OVERSIGHT, MANAGEMENT AND SANCTIONS > Management & Investigation
source_quote: g. consulting on and defining further regulatory provisions where there is a mandate papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:392

indicator_text: Advice, analysis and awareness raising: the oversight body should also offer guidance and training to lobbyists and public officials, analyse trends and report findings, raise awareness among the public and the profession, and promote best practice.
source_quote: Advice, analysis and awareness raising – this body or mechanism should also offer guidance and training to lobbyists and public officials on the application of relevant laws, analyse trends and report on their findings papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:394-397

indicator_text: Complaint mechanism: a well-publicised complaint mechanism that allows anyone to report violations openly, confidentially or anonymously, and to be informed on the specific outcome of the complaint, subject to privacy limitations.
source_quote: Complaint mechanism – there should be a well-publicised complaint mechanism that allows anyone to report violations either openly, confidentially or anonymously papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:399-402

indicator_text: Sanctions: lobbyists and public officials shall be subject to effective, proportionate and dissuasive sanctions for violations; sliding scale incl. criminal sanctions, (temporary) de-registration, and disciplinary proceedings; sanctioned names may be published; contracts concluded in violation may be deemed null and void; personal liability of leadership where they consented or connived.
source_quote: Sanctions – lobbyists and public officials shall be subject to effective, proportionate and dissuasive sanctions for the violation of lobbying rules. These sanctions should have a sliding scale papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:404-411

indicator_text: Local context: insofar as not contrary to the guiding principles, lobbying regulations should address the particularities of the local context — socio-political considerations (corporatism, formalised social partnerships); degree of informal and professional lobbying; nature of public concerns potentially giving rise to regulation.
source_quote: Local context – insofar as it is not contrary to the guiding principles, the lobbying regulations should address the particularities of the local context papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:421-425

indicator_text: Broader regulatory framework: design of lobbying regulations necessitates thorough review and consistency with — and potential amendment of — the broader regulatory framework, in particular laws/policies on trading-in-influence, political finance, public procurement, media law, labour law, whistle-blower protection, legislative procedure, judicial/administrative review, and rights to free speech/assembly/petition.
source_quote: Broader regulatory framework – the design of lobbying regulations necessitates a thorough review, consistency with and potential amendment of the broader regulatory framework papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:427-430
notes: Top-level standard #37 of 38. Composite indicator broken into 9 granular sub-points (2.a–2.i).

indicator_text: Trading in influence, bribery and other corrupt conduct
section_or_category: REGULATORY FRAMEWORK DESIGN > Broader regulatory framework
source_quote: a. trading in influence, bribery and other corrupt conduct papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:432

indicator_text: Political finance (limits and transparency) and sponsoring of election candidates or parties
section_or_category: REGULATORY FRAMEWORK DESIGN > Broader regulatory framework
source_quote: b. political finance (limits and transparency) and sponsoring of election candidates or parties papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:434-435

indicator_text: Public procurement and state benefits (due process and supervision mechanisms)
section_or_category: REGULATORY FRAMEWORK DESIGN > Broader regulatory framework
source_quote: c. public procurement and state benefits (due process and supervision mechanisms) papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:437

indicator_text: Media law (independence and sponsorship)
section_or_category: REGULATORY FRAMEWORK DESIGN > Broader regulatory framework
source_quote: d. media law (independence and sponsorship) papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:439

indicator_text: Labour law (collective bargaining)
section_or_category: REGULATORY FRAMEWORK DESIGN > Broader regulatory framework
source_quote: e. labour law (collective bargaining) papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:441

indicator_text: Whistle-blower protection
section_or_category: REGULATORY FRAMEWORK DESIGN > Broader regulatory framework
source_quote: f. whistle-blower protection papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:443

indicator_text: Legislative procedure (including bringing of items under urgency)
section_or_category: REGULATORY FRAMEWORK DESIGN > Broader regulatory framework
source_quote: g. legislative procedure (including bringing of items under urgency) papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:445

indicator_text: Judicial and administrative review
section_or_category: REGULATORY FRAMEWORK DESIGN > Broader regulatory framework
source_quote: h. judicial and administrative review papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:447

indicator_text: Rights to freedom of speech, assembly and petition of government
section_or_category: REGULATORY FRAMEWORK DESIGN > Broader regulatory framework
source_quote: i. rights to freedom of speech, assembly and petition of government papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:449

indicator_text: Periodic review: the regulatory framework should be subject to an initial review one year after commencement of the legislation, and thereafter further periodic review for effectiveness and compliance with guiding principles.
source_quote: Periodic review – the regulatory framework should be subject to an initial review one year after the commencement of the legislation and following that, further periodic review papers/text/AccessInfo_2015__intl_lobbying_standards.txt:451-453
